title: Serve can start locally and answer health checks
description: Black-box smoke for `armature serve` using a temporary isolated ARMATURE_HOME and a dynamically selected local port.
input: Start `armature serve` on a free local port, fetch `/health`, and verify the returned JSON still advertises an OK status and model metadata.
command: bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); PORT=$(node -e "const net=require(\"node:net\"); const s=net.createServer(); s.listen(0,\"127.0.0.1\",()=>{console.log(s.address().port); s.close();});"); env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ARMATURE_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.armature" ARMATURE_PROVIDER=openai OPENAI_API_KEY=test-openai-key node dist/bin/armature.js serve --port "$PORT" >"$TMP_HOME/serve.out" 2>&1 & SERVER_PID=$!; trap "kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null" EXIT; bash agent-eval/scripts/wait-for-http.sh "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/health" "^(200)$" 50 0.1; curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/health"'
